Off Calendar Apr-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Micah R. Jacobs, Esq. (415.445.4696; mjacobs@jacobslawsf.com) of Jacobs Law Group SF for Plaintiff.
J. James Li, Esq. (650.521.5956; lij@lilaw.us) of LiLaw Inc. for Defendants.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS DAVID SUN and MANXIN WANG'S Motion To Disqualify Attorney Micah Jacobs As Counsel For Automata Research Institute.

Defendants' (DAVID SUN and MANXIN WANG) Motion To Disqualify Attorney Micah Jacobs As Counsel For Automata Research Institute is denied. The party seeking disqualification has the burden of proof. (See Lynn v. George (2017) 15 Cal.App.5th 630, 638.) Defendants do not contend that attorney Jacobs has a conflict of interest based on prior or current representations. Rather, they contend that he cannot act for Automato Research Institute, Inc. because he was retained by persons that cannot properly act for that entity. The court concludes that a motion to disqualify counsel is not the proper vehicle to resolve the hotly-contested dispute regarding who can govern Automato, which is the heart of this case. Such a determination by motion raises due process considerations. (See Morrical v. Rogers (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 438, 459-460 [noting due process concerns in summary proceedings].) As the court previously noted in the motion for sanctions, the existence of the Termination and SAFE Agreements lend credence to plaintiff's position. Defendants have not convinced the court that plaintiff's invocation of Delaware Corporation Code, Section 108 was improper. (Shen Decl., par. 11.)

Order is signed.

Off Calendar Mar-28-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
MICAH R. JACOBS, Esq., JACOBS LAW GROUP SF, 415-445-4696, mjacobs@jacobslawsf.com for Plaintiff AUTOMATA RESEARCH INSTITUTE.
J. James Li, LILAW INC.,(650) 521-5956l ij@lilaw.us, for Defendant DAVID SUN, MANXIN WANG

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT DAVID SUN, MANXIN WANG Motion For Sanction Under Ccp 128.5

Defendants' (DAVID SUN and MANXIN WANG) motion for sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.5 is denied with prejudice. First, movants failed to comply with the safe harbor provision of CCP 128.5 by merely serving a notice of motion to trigger the safe harbor period and thereafter filing and serving a completely different motion that included legal authorities and evidence. (See Jacobs Decl. Exh. B.) Defendants' simple "notice of motion" is no different than the letters of intent to seek sanctions that courts have found do not comply with the safe harbor provision. In CPF Vaseo Associates, LLC v. Gray (2018) 29 Cal.App.5th 997, 1007, the court explains: "It should thus come as no surprise that 'informal notice of an intent to seek sanctions in the future cannot serve as a substitute to the requirements set forth in section 128.7 for a formal noticed motion. ' '[u]nder section 128.7, the papers to be served on the opposing party as notice of intent to file a section 128.7 sanctions motion must be the same papers that are ultimately filed with the court no less than 21 days later.'" The "motion" referenced in CCP 128.5(f)(1)(B) means a complete motion. Second, plaintiff presents sufficient evidence (e.g. the Termination and SAFE Agreements)

Order is signed.
